                                                                                                                                   Revision
 I Laplace transforms provide a mathematical tool to solve
   linear ODEs and systems of linear ODEs. In brief, the
   given ODE is transformed into an algebraic equation,
   which can be solved by purely algebraic manipulations.                                                                              I Integration by parts
   The solution to this latter equation is then transformed
   back to yield the solution to the ODE.                                                                                                                 Z b                      Z b
                                                                                                                                                                  u dv = uv|ba вИТ                v du.
 I Applications are in many areas of engineering and physics,                                                                                                 a                         a
   including signal processing, electrical networks, etc.
                                                                                                                                       I Definition of an improper integral:
Note                                                                                                                                                   Z вИЮ                         Z b
Laplace transforms involve integrals, in particular improper                                                                                                      f (t) dt = lim            f (t) dt.
integrals, which you should review in your own time.                                                                                                      a                 bвЖТвИЮ     a
Integration by parts and partial fractions are techniques
frequently used in this topic. You should consolidate your skills
on these techniques.

Rational functions                                                                                                              Example 2
                                                                                                                                Find the partial fractions expansions of the following functions:
    I A function f is a rational function if it is of the form
      f (x) = P (x)/Q(x) where P (x) and Q(x) are                                                                                       2x2 + 2x вИТ 6
      polynomials.                                                                                                               1.                       ;

    I A rational function f = P/Q is said to be proper if                                                                               8x + 9
                                                                                                                                 2.                 .
      deg P < deg Q.                                                                                                                (x вИТ 2)(x + 3)2

    I If a rational function f = P/Q is not proper, one can use
      long division to find polynomials p(x) and R(x) such that
      P (x) = p(x)Q(x) + R(x) with deg R < deg Q. Then f
      can be written as the sum of a polynomial and a proper
      rational function:

Laplace transforms

  Definition 3
  The Laplace transform of a function f : [0, вИЮ) вЖТ R is the
  function F defined by
                             Z вИЮ
                     F (s) =     eвИТst f (t) dt.
                               0

  In this case, we also write F = Lf = L{f (t)}.

  definition. What is the domain of F ?

                                                                                                                                    How to evaluate the Laplace transform in practice?

                                                                                                                                        I In practice we use a table of Laplace transforms, along
                                                                                                                                          with some transform theorems, to evaluate a given
                                                                                                                                          Laplace transform. One such table is provided in the
                                                                                                                                          tutorial problem booklet. It will also be provided in the
                                                                                                                                          final exam.
                                                                                                                                        I We will need the following definition, which extends the
                                                                                                                                          concept of factorial beyond the positive integers.
                                                                                                                                      Definition 9 (Gamma function)

   Example 12                                                                                                                Laplace transform is one-one
  Use the Table to find the Laplace transform of
                                                                                                                               The Laplace transform of the zero function f (t) вЙ° 0 is the
                                           вИЪ
                   f (t) = 3e7t вИТ 6t eвИТ2t + t.                                                                                 zero function F (s) вЙ° 0. In fact, the next theorem shows that
                                                                                                                               f (t) вЙ° 0 is the only function whose Laplace transform is
                                                                                                                               identically zero.

                                                                                                                               Theorem 13
                                                                                                                               Suppose that F (s) = L{f (t)}. If F (s) вЙ° 0 then f (t) вЙ° 0.

                                                                                                                               Now consider any two Laplace transforms F (s) = L{f (t)} and
                                                                                                                               G(s) = L{g(t)}. Let h(t) = f (t) вИТ g(t) then, by linearity, the
                                                                                                                               Laplace transform H(s) = L{h(t)} = F (s) вИТ G(s). If
                                                                                                                               F (s) вЙ° G(s), then the difference H(s) вЙ° 0 and, by the
                                                                                                                               Theorem, h(t) вЙ° 0. Hence, f (t) вЙ° g(t). In other words,

     3      6       ќУ(3/2)                                                                                                                      Lf = Lg     implies f = g.

Inverse Laplace transforms                                                                                                  Linearity of the inverse transform
                                                                                                                               Theorem 14
                                                                                                                               For any constants a and b,
    I Since the transformation L is one-one, it has an inverse
      transformation LвИТ1 . That is,                                                                                                   LвИТ1 {aF (s) + bG(s)} = aLвИТ1 F (s) + bLвИТ1 G(s).

              f (t) = LвИТ1 {F (s)}   iff F (s) = L{f (t)}.
                                                                                                                               Proof.
                                                                                                                               Let f (t) = LвИТ1 {F (s)} and g(t) = LвИТ1 {G(s)}. Since L is
       The notation f = LвИТ1 F is justified because f (t) is                                                                    linear,
       uniquely determined by F (s).
                                                                                                                                aF (s) + bG(s) = aL{f (t)} + bL{g(t)} = L{af (t) + bg(t)}
    I There is an explicit formula for LвИТ1 {F (s)} but it involves
      a contour integral in the complex plane. In practice, we                                                                 and therefore
      just use the Laplace transform table backwards together
      with some interesting tricks.                                                                                                LвИТ1 {aF (s) + bG(s)} = af (t) + bg(t)
                                                                                                                                                        = aLвИТ1 {F (s)} + bLвИТ1 {G(s)}.

                                                                                                   Effect of u(t вИТ a)

                                                                                                     Multiplying a function f (t) with u(t вИТ a) produces all sorts of
                                                                                                     effects. For example, the product f (t)u(t вИТ a) cuts the
                                                                                                     function f , whereas f (t вИТ a)u(t вИТ a) shifts and cuts.



                                                                                                     Example 21
                                                                                                     Let f (t) = t2 . Sketch each of the following functions.
                                                                                                      1. g(t) = f (t)u(t вИТ 4) (This is a cut.)
                                                                                                      2. g(t) = f (t вИТ 4)u(t вИТ 4) (This is a shift plus cut.)
                                                                                                                                            
